major_arc_linear_lower_bound.statement: for all even n > majorArcThreshold, coefficient * n + minorArcError(n) <= rawVonMangoldtFourierMajorArcContribution(majorArcs, n)

minor_arc_uniform_dft_bound_valid.statement: for all even n > minorArcThreshold and k outside majorArcs(n), |vonMangoldtZModDft(n,k)| <= minorArcDftBound(n)

minor_arc_dft_bound_sq_error_bound.statement: for all even n > minorArcThreshold, minorArcDftBound(n)^2 <= minorArcError(n)

contamination_dominated.statement: for all even n > contaminationThreshold, 2 * vonMangoldtWeightSumContaminationBudget(canonicalNonPrimePrimePowerVonMangoldtWeightSumBound, n) < coefficient * n

contamination_sqrt_log_model_bound.statement: for all even n > contaminationThreshold, vonMangoldtSqrtLogBudgetComparisonConstant * sqrt(n) * log(n)^3 < coefficient * n

notes: Template only. This is the direct DFT positive-linear route with an explicit contamination threshold: the analytic side supplies a lower bound for the real major-frequency contribution, a uniform minor DFT estimate with M(n)^2 <= minorArcError(n), and explicit domination of the canonical prime-power contamination budget. The minor DFT bound does not need a separate nonnegativity declaration, because the Lean bridge uses the squared uniform bound directly. If contaminationThreshold >= 2, the optional contamination_sqrt_log_model_bound can derive contamination_dominated through the formalized sqrt(n)*log(n)^3 comparison theorem. Lean derives the final threshold from majorArcThreshold, minorArcThreshold, and contaminationThreshold, avoiding the noncomputable canonicalLinearContaminationThreshold.
